info.description:
Apache Airflow is a platform for programmatically authoring, scheduling, and monitoring workflows as directed acyclic graphs (DAGs).

Authentication: HTTP Basic — Airflow username and password. Configure via the Airflow UI under Admin → Users.

Source: https://airflow.apache.org/docs/apache-airflow/stable/stable-rest-api-ref.html
info.title: Apache Airflow
info.x-itential-curated: Trimmed to 50 of 73 upstream operations covering common CRUD for automation: DAGs, DAG Runs, Task Instances (incl. XCom entries), Connections, Variables, and Pools, plus Health/Version. See the repo README for the full scope and the full spec.

get.description: Get a source code using file token.

List DAGs in the database.
`dag_id_pattern` can be set to match dags of a specific pattern

parameters.description: If set, only return DAGs with dag_ids matching this pattern.
parameters.name: dag_id_pattern

patch.description:
Update DAGs of a given dag_id_pattern using UpdateMask.
This endpoint allows specifying `~` as the dag_id_pattern to update all DAGs.
*New in version 2.3.0*

parameters.description: If set, only update DAGs with dag_ids matching this pattern.
parameters.name: dag_id_pattern

delete.description:
Deletes all metadata related to the DAG, including finished DAG Runs and Tasks.
Logs are not deleted. This action cannot be undone.

*New in version 2.2.0*

get.description:
Presents only information available in database (DAGModel).
If you need detailed information, consider using GET /dags/{dag_id}/details.
get.summary: Get basic information about a DAG

post.description: Clears a set of task instances associated with the DAG for a specified date range.
post.summary: Clear a set of task instances

get.description: This endpoint allows specifying `~` as the dag_id to retrieve DAG runs for all DAGs.

*New in version 2.4.0*

patch.description:

*New in version 2.5.0*

get.description: This endpoint allows specifying `~` as the dag_id, dag_run_id to retrieve DAG runs for all DAGs and DAG runs.

patch.description:
Updates the state for single task instance.
*New in version 2.5.0*
patch.summary: Updates the state of a task instance

get.description: List extra links for task instance.

Get details of all mapped task instances.

*New in version 2.3.0*
Get logs for a specific task instance and its try number.

patch.description:
Update the manual user note of a non-mapped Task Instance.

*New in version 2.5.0*

requestBody.description: Parameters of set Task Instance note.

get.description: This endpoint allows specifying `~` as the dag_id, dag_run_id, task_id to retrieve XCOM entries for for all DAGs, DAG runs and task instances. XCom values won't be returned as they can be large. Use this endpoint to get a list of XCom entries and then fetch individual entry to get value.

parameters.description:
Whether to deserialize an XCom value when using a custom XCom backend.

The XCom API endpoint calls `orm_deserialize_value` by default since an XCom may contain value
that is potentially expensive to deserialize in the web server. Setting this to true overrides
the consideration, and calls `deserialize_value` instead.

This parameter is not meaningful when using the default XCom backend.

*New in version 2.4.0*
parameters.name: deserialize

get.description:
Get details of a mapped task instance.

*New in version 2.3.0*

*New in version 2.5.0*
patch.summary: Updates the state of a mapped task instance

patch.description:
Update the manual user note of a mapped Task Instance.

requestBody.description: Parameters of set Task Instance note.

get.description: The response contains many DAG attributes, so the response can be large. If possible, consider using GET /dags/{dag_id}.
get.summary: Get a simplified representation of DAG
Get simplified representation of a task

post.description: Updates the state for multiple task instances simultaneously.
post.summary: Set a state of task instances

post.description: This endpoint is a POST to allow filtering across a large number of DAG IDs, where as a GET it would run in to maximum HTTP request URL length limit.

List task instances from all DAGs and DAG runs.
This endpoint is a POST to allow filtering across a large number of DAG IDs, where as a GET it would run in to maximum HTTP request URL length limits.

get.description:
Get the status of Airflow's metadatabase and scheduler. It includes info about
metadatabase and last heartbeat of scheduler.
The collection does not contain data. To get data, you must get a single entity.

dry_run.description:
If set, don't actually run this operation. The response will contain a list of task instances
planned to be cleaned, but not modified in any way.

dag_run_id.description: The DagRun ID for this task instance

dry_run.description:
If set, don't actually run this operation. The response will contain a list of task instances
planned to be cleaned, but not modified in any way.

end_date.description: The maximum execution date to clear.

include_downstream.description: If set to true, downstream tasks are also affected.

include_future.description: If set to True, also tasks from future DAG Runs are affected.

include_parentdag.description: Clear tasks in the parent dag of the subdag.

include_past.description: If set to True, also tasks from past DAG Runs are affected.

include_subdags.description: Clear tasks in subdags and clear external tasks indicated by ExternalTaskMarker.

include_upstream.description: If set to true, upstream tasks are also affected.

reset_dag_runs.description: Set state of DAG runs to RUNNING.

start_date.description: The minimum execution date to clear.

task_ids.description:
A list of task ids to clear.

*New in version 2.1.0*

total_entries.description: Count of objects in the current result set.

extra.description: Other values that cannot be put into another field, e.g. RSA keys.

ConnectionCollection.description:
Collection of connections.

*Changed in version 2.1.0*: 'total_entries' field is added.

ConnectionCollectionItem.description:
Connection collection item.
The password and extra fields are only available when retrieving a single object due to the sensitivity of this data.

message.description: The success or failure message of the request.

default_view.description:
Default view of the DAG inside the webserver

*New in version 2.3.0*

description.description: User-provided DAG description, which can consist of several sentences or paragraphs that describe DAG contents.

file_token.description: The key containing the encrypted path to the file. Encryption and decryption take place only on the server. This prevents the client from reading an non-DAG file. This also ensures API extensibility, because the format of encrypted data may change.

fileloc.description: The absolute path to the file.

has_import_errors.description:
Whether the DAG has import errors

*New in version 2.3.0*

has_task_concurrency_limits.description:
Whether the DAG has task concurrency limits

*New in version 2.3.0*

is_active.description:
Whether the DAG is currently seen by the scheduler(s).

*New in version 2.1.1*

*Changed in version 2.2.0*: Field is read-only.

last_expired.description:
Time when the DAG last received a refresh signal
(e.g. the DAG's "refresh" button was clicked in the web UI)

*New in version 2.3.0*

last_parsed_time.description:
The last time the DAG was parsed.

*New in version 2.3.0*

last_pickled.description:
The last time the DAG was pickled.

*New in version 2.3.0*

max_active_runs.description:
Maximum number of active DAG runs for the DAG

*New in version 2.3.0*

max_active_tasks.description:
Maximum number of active tasks that can be run on the DAG

*New in version 2.3.0*

next_dagrun.description:
The logical date of the next dag run.

*New in version 2.3.0*

next_dagrun_create_after.description:
Earliest time at which this ``next_dagrun`` can be created.

*New in version 2.3.0*

next_dagrun_data_interval_end.description:
The end of the interval of the next dag run.

*New in version 2.3.0*

next_dagrun_data_interval_start.description:
The start of the interval of the next dag run.

*New in version 2.3.0*

pickle_id.description:
Foreign key to the latest pickle_id

*New in version 2.3.0*

root_dag_id.description: If the DAG is SubDAG then it is the top level DAG identifier. Otherwise, null.

scheduler_lock.description:
Whether (one of) the scheduler is scheduling this DAG at the moment

*New in version 2.3.0*

DAGCollection.description:
Collection of DAGs.

*Changed in version 2.1.0*: 'total_entries' field is added.

end_date.description:
The DAG's end date.

*New in version 2.3.0*.

is_paused_upon_creation.description:
Whether the DAG is paused upon creation.

*New in version 2.3.0*

last_parsed.description:
The last time the DAG was parsed.

*New in version 2.3.0*

render_template_as_native_obj.description:
Whether to render templates as native Python objects.

*New in version 2.3.0*

start_date.description:
The DAG's start date.

*Changed in version 2.0.1*: Field becomes nullable.

template_search_path.description:
The template search path.

*New in version 2.3.0*

conf.description:
JSON object describing additional configuration parameters.

The value of this field can be set only when creating the object. If you try to modify the
field of an existing object, the request fails with an BAD_REQUEST error.

dag_run_id.description:
Run ID.

The value of this field can be set only when creating the object. If you try to modify the
field of an existing object, the request fails with an BAD_REQUEST error.

If not provided, a value will be generated based on execution_date.

If the specified dag_run_id is in use, the creation request fails with an ALREADY_EXISTS error.

This together with DAG_ID are a unique key.

execution_date.description:
The execution date. This is the same as logical_date, kept for backwards compatibility.
If both this field and logical_date are provided but with different values, the request
will fail with an BAD_REQUEST error.

*Changed in version 2.2.0*: Field becomes nullable.

*Deprecated since version 2.2.0*: Use 'logical_date' instead.

logical_date.description:
The logical date (previously called execution date). This is the time or interval covered by
this DAG run, according to the DAG definition.

The value of this field can be set only when creating the object. If you try to modify the
field of an existing object, the request fails with an BAD_REQUEST error.

This together with DAG_ID are a unique key.

*New in version 2.2.0*

note.description:
Contains manually entered notes by the user about the DagRun.

*New in version 2.5.0*

start_date.description:
The start time. The time when DAG run was actually created.

*Changed in version 2.1.3*: Field becomes nullable.

DAGRunCollection.description:
Collection of DAG runs.

*Changed in version 2.1.0*: 'total_entries' field is added.

DagState.description:
DAG State.

*Changed in version 2.1.3*: 'queued' is added as a possible value.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.

instance.description:
A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem. It may or may
not yield further information if dereferenced.

status.description: The HTTP status code generated by the API server for this occurrence of the problem.

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem type.

type.description:
A URI reference [RFC3986] that identifies the problem type. This specification
encourages that, when dereferenced, it provide human-readable documentation for
the problem type.

ExtraLink.description: Additional links containing additional information about the task.

dag_ids.description:
Return objects with specific DAG IDs.
The value can be repeated to retrieve multiple matching values (OR condition).

end_date_gte.description:
Returns objects greater or equal the specified date.

This can be combined with end_date_lte parameter to receive only the selected period.

end_date_lte.description:
Returns objects less than or equal to the specified date.

This can be combined with end_date_gte parameter to receive only the selected period.

execution_date_gte.description:
Returns objects greater or equal to the specified date.

This can be combined with execution_date_lte key to receive only the selected period.

execution_date_lte.description:
Returns objects less than or equal to the specified date.

This can be combined with execution_date_gte key to receive only the selected period.

order_by.description:
The name of the field to order the results by. Prefix a field name
with `-` to reverse the sort order.

*New in version 2.1.0*

page_limit.description: The numbers of items to return.

page_offset.description: The number of items to skip before starting to collect the result set.

start_date_gte.description:
Returns objects greater or equal the specified date.

This can be combined with start_date_lte key to receive only the selected period.

start_date_lte.description:
Returns objects less or equal the specified date.

This can be combined with start_date_gte parameter to receive only the selected period

states.description:
Return objects with specific states.
The value can be repeated to retrieve multiple matching values (OR condition).

dag_ids.description:
Return objects with specific DAG IDs.
The value can be repeated to retrieve multiple matching values (OR condition).

duration_gte.description:
Returns objects greater than or equal to the specified values.

This can be combined with duration_lte parameter to receive only the selected period.

duration_lte.description:
Returns objects less than or equal to the specified values.

This can be combined with duration_gte parameter to receive only the selected range.

end_date_gte.description:
Returns objects greater or equal the specified date.

This can be combined with start_date_lte parameter to receive only the selected period.

end_date_lte.description:
Returns objects less than or equal to the specified date.

execution_date_gte.description:
Returns objects greater or equal to the specified date.

execution_date_lte.description:
Returns objects less than or equal to the specified date.

This can be combined with execution_date_gte parameter to receive only the selected period.

pool.description: The value can be repeated to retrieve multiple matching values (OR condition).

queue.description: The value can be repeated to retrieve multiple matching values (OR condition).

Returns objects greater or equal the specified date.

This can be combined with start_date_lte parameter to receive only the selected period.

start_date_lte.description:
Returns objects less or equal the specified date.

state.description: The value can be repeated to retrieve multiple matching values (OR condition).

description.description:
The description of the pool.

*New in version 2.3.0*

occupied_slots.description: The number of slots used by running/queued tasks at the moment.

open_slots.description: The number of free slots at the moment.

queued_slots.description: The number of slots used by queued tasks at the moment.

slots.description: The maximum number of slots that can be assigned to tasks. One job may occupy one or more slots.

used_slots.description: The number of slots used by running tasks at the moment.

PoolCollection.description:
Collection of pools.

*Changed in version 2.1.0*: 'total_entries' field is added.

ScheduleInterval.description:
Schedule interval. Defines how often DAG runs, this object gets added to your latest task instance's
execution_date to figure out the next schedule.

SchedulerStatus.description: The status and the latest scheduler heartbeat.

latest_scheduler_heartbeat.description: The time the scheduler last do a heartbeat.

note.description: Custom notes left by users for this Dag Run.
The custom note to set for this Task Instance.

dag_run_id.description:
The DagRun ID for this task instance

*New in version 2.3.0*

note.description:
Contains manually entered notes by the user about the TaskInstance.

*New in version 2.5.0*

rendered_fields.description:
JSON object describing rendered fields.

*New in version 2.3.0*

TaskInstanceCollection.description:
Collection of task instances.

*Changed in version 2.1.0*: 'total_entries' field is added.

TaskState.description:
Task state.

*Changed in version 2.0.2*: 'removed' is added as a possible value.

*Changed in version 2.2.0*: 'deferred' is added as a possible value.

*Changed in version 2.4.0*: 'sensing' state has been removed.
*Changed in version 2.4.2*: 'restarting' is added as a possible value

TriggerRule.description:
Trigger rule.

*Changed in version 2.2.0*: 'none_failed_min_one_success' is added as a possible value.

UpdateDagRunState.description:
Modify the state of a DAG run.

*New in version 2.2.0*

state.description: The state to set this DagRun

dry_run.description:
If set, don't actually run this operation. The response will contain the task instance
planned to be affected, but won't be modified in any way.

dag_run_id.description:
The task instance's DAG run ID. Either set this or execution_date but not both.

*New in version 2.3.0*

dry_run.description:
If set, don't actually run this operation. The response will contain a list of task instances
planned to be affected, but won't be modified in any way.

execution_date.description: The execution date. Either set this or dag_run_id but not both.

include_downstream.description: If set to true, downstream tasks are also affected.

include_future.description: If set to True, also tasks from future DAG Runs are affected.

include_past.description: If set to True, also tasks from past DAG Runs are affected.

include_upstream.description: If set to true, upstream tasks are also affected.

VariableCollection.description:
Collection of variables.

*Changed in version 2.1.0*: 'total_entries' field is added.

VariableCollectionItem.description:
XCom entry collection item.
The value field are only available when retrieving a single object due to the sensitivity of this data.

description.description:
The description of the variable.

*New in version 2.4.0*

git_version.description: The git version (including git commit hash)

XComCollection.description:
Collection of XCom entries.

*Changed in version 2.1.0*: 'total_entries' field is added.

XComCollectionItem.description:
XCom entry collection item.

The value field is only available when reading a single object due to the size of the value.

ContinuationToken.description:
A token that allows you to continue fetching logs.
If passed, it will specify the location from which the download should be continued.
ContinuationToken.name: token

FileToken.description:
The key containing the encrypted path to the file. Encryption and decryption take place only on
the server. This prevents the client from reading an non-DAG file. This also ensures API
extensibility, because the format of encrypted data may change.
FileToken.name: file_token

AlreadyExists.description: An existing resource conflicts with the request.

NotAcceptable.description: A specified Accept header is not allowed.

NotFound.description: A specified resource is not found.

PermissionDenied.description: Client does not have sufficient permission.

Unauthenticated.description: Request not authenticated due to missing, invalid, authentication info.
